name: Zucchini-And-Tomato Gratin
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion; quartered and thinly sliced
1/2 teaspoon Herbes de Provence (or a mixture of chopped rosemary, thyme, and sage)
1 1/2 pounds summer squash; sliced into ovals that are 1/4 inch thick
1/2 pound tomatoes; sliced into rounds (Roma, plum, or large cherry)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Warm one tablespoon of olive oil in a large pan and add the onion, garlic, and half the Herbes de Provence. Salt lightly and sauté gently for about five minutes.
3. Remove the onions with tongs and spread them evenly over the bottom of a gratin dish. Season with pepper.
4. Warm another tablespoon of olive oil in the same skillet, increase the heat to medium-high, and add the zucchini. Add a few pinches of salt and the remaining Herbes de Provence. Sauté, stirring occasionally, until they start to color, about 10 minutes.
5. Distribute the zucchini over the onions; arrange them casually. Tuck in the tomato slices here and there, along with the olives.
6. Drizzle the remaining oil over the top of the gratin.
7. Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes.
8. Remove the foil and continue to bake another 10 minutes or until the squash is tender and the juices are reduced. Allow to cool before serving warm or tepid.
